title: synaphea spinulosa subsp. major
intro: Synaphea spinulosa subsp. major, also known as the spiny synaphea, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Frankeniaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is known to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Synaphea spinulosa subsp. major is traditionally used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
